item.description: To be clear: I don't get the sense that the Bucks don't want Herro. I've actually heard that Milwaukee and Herro have mutual interest. But the Bucks will clearly be trending toward a total reset from the Antetokounmpo Era if they proceed with a trade over the next few days. If they can get additional future assets by shipping Herro to Detroit or elsewhere ... how could they resist?

item.title: Bobby Marks: I'm still in the belief it's Miami …
item.description: Bobby Marks: I'm still in the belief it's Miami because when you look at Giannis Antetokounmpo, it's the superstar checklist of three things that Miami has here: It's the win-now player. When you look at a player like Tyler Herro, remember you've got to get to $47-48 million in salary just to send out. Herro makes $33 million as an expiring contract. Then you look at young players like Kel'el Ware Ware and Jaime Jaquez, two players on rookie deals. And then it's the draft picks. Remember, Milwau

item.title: There's not been any indication that Giannis doesn't …
item.description: Sam Amick: I think the issue with Minnesota is that they have not been given any indication. There's two things. There's not been any indication that Giannis doesn't want to be there long term, but I don't get the sense that he's giving them the sign that he does want to be there. And that is another thing that as we get closer to draft day, I'm wondering with Giannis and his camp, are they going to step on the scale even more than they have to this point? Because right now, it's kind of a loose

item.title: If Giannis signs the extension with the Bucks simply …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I feel safe in saying nobody is going to top a Heat offer if Giannis makes clear he won't sign extension anywhere but Miami. The Bucks then would have 2 options: 1) Succumb and accept Heat's offer. 2). Call Giannis' bluff, keep him and gamble that he won't turn down a 4-year, $275 M extension on Oct. 1. If Giannis signs the extension with the Bucks simply to guarantee more financial security, but then asks for a trade weeks later, it would be very amusing. BUT... the trade market

item.title: If Giannis signed the Bucks extension Oct. 1 and then …
item.description: Barry Jackson: If Giannis signed the Bucks extension Oct. 1 and then asked for a trade, the Bucks could trade him Oct. 1, 2027 (12 months later, before the 2027-28 season). But everybody would just be playing games with each other for a year.

item.title: I was told a short time ago the Celtics have …
item.description: Gery Woelfel: I was told a short time ago the Celtics have intensified their efforts to land Antetokounmpo. The source declined to get into the details. Another league source said he was told the Celtics were more receptive to moving star Jaylen Brown, although it’s unlikely he wound wind up in Milwaukee but rather with a third team. While the Celtics appear focused on landing Antetokounmpo, it is generally believed Miami remains the frontrunner for the two-time MVP.

item.title: Bucks would consider retaining Jaylen Brown if they land him in Giannis Antetokounmpo deal
item.description: Despite some executives around the league assuming it would be a foregone conclusion that the Bucks would try to flip Brown to another team to get future first-round draft pick compensation as part of a rebuilding effort, it’s not a certainty. If the Celtics indeed acquire Antetokounmpo and Brown ends up going to the Bucks as part of the trade, the Bucks would consider keeping Brown, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Portland has discussed trying to acquire …
item.description: The Portland Trail Blazers have been involved in Antetokounmpo trade talks on multiple fronts. Portland has discussed trying to acquire Antetokounmpo, and as a potential facilitator because they have two future first-round Bucks swaps in 2028 and 2030, league sources said.

item.title: It’s worth noting that Nikola Jovic, Kasparas …
item.description: It’s worth noting that Nikola Jovic, Kasparas Jakucionis, and Pelle Larsson have each come up in different iterations of the Antetokounmpo trade talks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Other teams have always called on rising swingman …
item.description: Other teams have always called on rising swingman Jaden McDaniels. Minnesota has made him essentially untradable unless it was for a blockbuster trade involving Giannis Antetokounmpo or Nikola Jokic. When the Los Angeles Clippers were struggling earlier this season amid a 6-21 start and gauging the trade market for Kawhi Leonard and James Harden each, the Clippers poked around on McDaniels, but were rebuffed, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: The Bucks, incidentally, are VERY much aware of how …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Bucks, incidentally, are VERY much aware of how much Giannis wants to play in Miami. They seem determined to leave the Heat in a compromised roster position so that the Heat also has a hard time doing what the Bucks couldn't do the past 5 years (build a championship team around him). That's the reason I've been saying that it serves Giannis' interest to make clear to the world that he will sign an extension only with Miami, thus dissuading other potential bidders. That could a

item.title: Amsterdam native and Albany Academy alumnus Andre …
item.description: A local NBA star is coming home to host a basketball camp next weekend. Amsterdam native and Albany Academy alumnus Andre Jackson Jr. and Foundation 44 are hosting a basketball clinic on Saturday, June 27th.

item.title: Celtics all in on Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Boston Celtics are going ALL IN on Giannis Antetokounmpo and Jaylen Brown is on the table, per @WindhorstESPN “If Giannis is traded in the next 24 which I think is a good chance of happening, I think it’s going to be Boston or Miami…. Jaylen Brown is on the table”

item.title: If there is a Heat deal, it likely will be completed …
item.description: Ira Winderman: This never was going to be easy. If there is a Heat deal, it likely will be completed later, due to cap/calendar rules. If there is not, then it would seem the time for Pat Riley to talk.

item.title: "It really is something of a dream come true," …
item.description: After his debut NBA season, Miami Heat rookie Kasparas Jakucionis returned to Lithuania to fulfill one of his dreams – making his national team debut. "It really is something of a dream come true," Jakucionis told reporters in Lithuania's national team training camp in Birstonas. "I always watched the national team and always wanted to be part of the national team. I'm really very happy."

item.title: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed …
item.description: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed this spring includes the Milwaukee Bucks’ Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Dallas Mavericks’ Kyrie Irving, the New Orleans Pelicans’ Trey Murphy III, the Chicago Bulls’ Josh Giddey, the Boston Celtics’ Derrick White and many more. A couple of important caveats are that every team in the league is considering veteran help, and that having interest in a player does not mean any potential trades have ever been close.

item.title: Celtics have real shot at Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: One league source, after my story published, told @TheSteinLine that the Celtics emerged from the weekend “with a real shot” to win this race with a Jaylen Brown-centric offer … adding that Milwaukee has considered going ahead even without a third-team facilitator.

item.title: "If you force me to make a choice, yes. But I'm really …
item.description: With Antetokounmpo plagued by injuries and limited to just 36 games this past season, the Milwaukee Bucks appear ready to move on and initiate a rebuild. When asked if the trade will happen in the next 24 hours, Stein said: "If you force me to make a choice, yes. But I'm really taking my cues from every team I talk to, and every agent I talk to, and the answer comes back 'yes' every time".

item.title: Celtics have not given up hope on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: League sources say that the Heat remain in full-speed pursuit of Antetokounmpo but also indicate that the Boston Celtics have not yet abandoned hope of completing a trade for The Greek Freak. They are the two teams, sources say, that have engaged in the most advanced trade talks to date with the Bucks.

item.title: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the …
item.description: Heat Central: Chad Johnson is doubling down on his sources saying Giannis is coming to Miami: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the months. The sources that I do have, they know what they’re talking about. Which is why I’ve came on here and say things that I’ve said. All the other stuff, with Boston, and him going anywhere, none of that is going to happen. That’s just created dialogue to keep people guessing on where he might go.”

item.title: Bucks interested in Hugo Gonzalez
item.description: The Celtics, like Miami, would naturally be asked to add players and future draft compensation to the ultimate trade construction. Sources say that the Bucks have interest in Boston's prized young Spanish guard Hugo Gonzalez.

item.title: The Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent …
item.description: Is Minnesota still in the hunt? Not as of Friday. That's when The Stein Line revealed in the piece below that the Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent Antetokounmpo trade suitors in February, quietly "circled back to the Bucks this week to see what it would take to make another run at pairing Antetokounmpo with Anthony Edwards." Only for the Wolves to be informed, league sources say, that the Bucks want "even more now than the teams discussed in the winter."

item.title: Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the …
item.description: Contrary to recent reports and speculation, the Los Angeles Lakers appear not to be in any potential trade frameworks that would help complete the Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster trade. Appearing on ALL NBA Podcast, seasoned NBA insider Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the ongoing Giannis trade sweepstakes. "I have to be honest based on what I’ve heard. I haven’t heard that," Stein revealed about LA potentially being involved. "I know there was all kinds of speculation yesterd

item.title: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. Dallas is telling teams Kyrie Irving is not available. The Pistons have expressed interest in Trey Murphy, but so have a zillion other teams, and New Orleans' threshold for trading Trey Murphy away is really, really high. So the Pistons have looked at other targets. Tyler Herro, if he ends up going to Milwaukee in a Giannis trade, we reported that Thursday night, we revealed that, that the Pistons have interest in Tyler Herro, but

item.title: Shams: If Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee
item.description: Heat Central: “All I’ll say to you Greeny, if the Milwaukee Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee” — @ShamsCharania says Jaylen Brown will be staying in MIL after a Giannis trade

item.title: “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo WILL BE traded before the draft and is focused on Boston or Miami, per @ShamsCharania “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, it’s going to be a one for one deal. It’s going to consist of MIL and one of these two teams. There’s no 3rd and 4th teams.”

item.title: “I want to tell you that process is already underway. …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have ALREADY been trying to identify players to add after a Giannis trade, per @WindhorstESPN “I want to tell you that process is already underway. They have already identified players that they can chase if they get this deal done.”

item.title: Giannis is content with either of these two …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Per Shams: Giannis is expected to be traded before tomorrow’s draft Likely headed to either Boston (who have made Jaylen Brown available) or Miami Giannis is content with either of these two destinations (willing to sign extension)

item.title: Gary Harris exercises player option with Milwaukee
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: Gary Harris has picked up his $3.8M player option with the Milwaukee Bucks for the 2026-27 season, a league source told @spotrac.com .

item.title: Jaylen Brown to be traded regardless of whether Celtics land Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst believes Jaylen Brown will be traded even if the Celtics miss on Giannis and is accepting that reality: “I believe if the Bucks choose the Miami Heat offer, Jaylen Brown will get traded somewhere else. They are going to move on here…. From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizing it’s a chance to turn the page and may be getting his own team”

item.title: Kyle Kuzma a few minutes ago..
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Kyle Kuzma a few minutes ago... The Giannis trade is getting close. #NBA

item.title: Bucks looking to remain competitive as they discuss Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: League sources say the Bucks want to remain competitive next season. They don’t outright own any of their firsts until 2031, and even if they did, the flattened draft odds don’t incentivize teams to bottom out. And the best player on the table is, by far, Jaylen Brown.

item.title: Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would …
item.description: League sources say the Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would be attached to Brown: the 27th pick? How many future firsts? Hugo Gonzalez? Payton Pritchard? The Celtics have been reluctant to give up much more than Brown.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo willing to sign extension with Celtics if traded
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo…Celtics are in the mix. Giannis’ camp has indicated he’d sign extension with Boston. But it remains unclear how close the Cs and Bucks are to a potential deal.

item.title: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis Antetokounmpo thing at different points. They're not involved right now. What happens there? They know they need to make an upgrade, they know they need to add something to this team with Anthony Edwards on it. They've made a pitch on a bunch of different guys around the league. We'll see who they can come up with, but there's going to be a lot of tentacles that come out."

item.title: Celtics not willing to add multiple firsts for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Bucks owners are “split” on which decision to make with a Giannis trade and Boston is NOT willing to add multiple 1st round picks like Miami, per @JakeLFischer “We’re being told there is some type of split faction in MIL right now among the major stakeholders for the Bucks… the Celtics to our understanding, are not willing to offer the same level of multiple years of draft capital that the Miami Heat are” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: Raptors could be interested in Myles Turner?
item.description: A couple of league sources have suggested that the Raptors could be interested in Bucks centre Myles Turner if the Bucks go into fire-sale mode after a potential Antetokounmpo trade.

item.title: GOD, I trusted you at the beginning, and I will …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: GOD, I trusted you at the beginning, and I will continue to trust you throughout.

item.title: He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis is on his way back from the states and ready for a trade to Miami or Boston, per @ShamsCharania "At this very hour the MIL Bucks are moving closer and closer to a trade resolution for Giannis.... He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of right now. He's ready for a trade whether it's Miami or Boston"

item.title: The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Bucks, as we reported last night, have certainly asked for Hugo. The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep him. Not exactly a make-or-break element but one of the variables that factors into this Boston vs. Miami struggle.

item.title: Since when is a 6’9 wing who plays any role you ask, …
item.description: kuz: Since when is a 6’9 wing who plays any role you ask, guards 1–5, shot almost 50% from the field and cares about defense a ‘throw‑in’? lol

item.title: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites to acquire Giannis tonight or tomorrow

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade may expand before July 6
item.description: The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand. Last June, the Phoenix Suns and Houston Rockets agreed to a deal that ultimately increased to a historic seven-team trade.

item.title: The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at …
item.description: The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: the Heat and Boston Celtics, who were both on Antetokounmpo's list of preferred destinations. The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline in February, and aggressively pursued him this time around, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, according to sources.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo's camp told Bucks he would not sign a long-term extension with the team
item.description: Sources told ESPN that Antetokounmpo and his agent, Alex Saratsis, consistently informed the Bucks from May 2025 through May 2026 that the franchise legend wanted a trade from the organization because he believed it was best for both sides to part ways. Antetokounmpo's reps informed the Bucks several times over the past 13 months -- including before, during and after the season -- that he would not sign another long-term extension with the team, according to sources. The Bucks made the decision

item.title: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's Hugo Gonzalez hang-up. Had been hearing all day the Heat did not want to part with the Illinois product on top of their Herro-Ware-Jacquez-No. 13 structure. That's an All-Star guard, three recent first-round picks still on rookie deals, a second lottery pick in tomorrow's loaded draft class, plus two more future firsts and a swap... It's really a sizable haul for Giannis Antetokounmpo. Compared to Jaylen Brown and two first-round

item.title: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks that they're getting Giannis. Cautious optimism pays off lol. Miami is exactly where Giannis preferred to be, someone who works for him reiterated again today.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on January 6 to sign an extension for: 4 years/$275M if he opts-out 3 years/$214M if he opts-in The opt-in is slightly more lucrative

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo heads to Miami in six player deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BLOCKBUSTER: The Milwaukee Bucks are trading franchise icon Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis to the Miami Heat for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, 3 first-round picks (including No. 13), 1 pick swap and 1 second-rounder, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected first-rounders in 2031 and 2033 along with No. 13, a pick swap in 2030 and a 2033 second-rounder, according to sources. The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand.

item.title: Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: Bucks didn't want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee?
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Sources say Bucks owner Jimmy Haslem was a driving force in taking the Heat deal for Giannis Antetokounmpo over Boston’s offer. Haslem didn’t want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee in a year after dealing with Giannis and Myles Garrett trade demands. Haslem wanted certainty, and the Miami deal provided more of that with more total assets in return to Milwaukee.

item.title: The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team Giannis Antetokounmpo trade framework. They'd create a massive $25.5 million trade exception, which could grow if they expand the trade. It would be useful for taking on bad salaries with draft picks to add to their surplus.

item.title: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign …
item.description: Barry Jackson: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign multiyear deal with Miami instead - and if Norman Powell re-signs - that's a rotation of Giannis, Bam, Mitchell, Powell, Wiggins, with Larsson, Bobby Portis and player/players acquired through 3 different exceptions. Pretty, pretty good.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo is the first multi-time MVP …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: FWIW: Giannis Antetokounmpo is the first multi-time MVP winner traded away since Moses Malone.

item.title: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s. The 2030 swap with the Heat could potentially offset the swap they already owe to the Trail Blazers. They could really use more second-round picks.

item.title: Giannis only cares about two things: basketball and …
item.description: As I said, Giannis only cares about two things: basketball and family. That’s it. So if you respect his family, and how private he tries to keep things, and also respect the game of basketball, it feels like you’re going to be in a good spot. Very early in my career, I realized that Antetokounmpo greatly values the opportunity to speak for himself. There is little value in pontificating on what he might be thinking or feeling because he would much prefer to tell you himself. He says what he beli

item.title: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say …
item.description: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say it remains unclear if Tyler Herro will remain with the team. While there is interest in making the Milwaukee native part of the his hometown’s program, sources say the Bucks will also listen to offers for the 26-year-old former All-Star. Herro is believed to have a robust market, with the Detroit Pistons known to be among the many teams interested in him. Since this trade won’t be finalized until July 6, it remains possible for additional te

item.title: Heat attempted to include Davion Mitchell in Giannis trade
item.description: Per league sources, the Heat attempted to include point guard Davion Mitchell in the deal instead of Jakucionis. However, because Mitchell is entering the final year of his deal, as are Herro and Jaquez Jr., the Bucks made it clear that they had no interest in three of the four players in the deal having one season remaining. So the Bucks pushed for, and landed, Jakucionis. The 20-year-old, 6-foot-5 guard has three guaranteed seasons left on his rookie deal, including two team options.

item.title: “One guy that I already heard attached to them is …
item.description: Heat Central: Khris Middleton has already been linked to the Miami Heat, per @WindhorstESPN. “One guy that I already heard attached to them is Khris Middleton. Who obviously has a long history with Giannis and Bobby Portis… I don’t think you’re looking at the Heat instantaneously as title contenders”

item.title: Haynes: Tyler Herro thrilled about fresh start
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: Tyler Herro is thrilled about a fresh start and playing for his hometown team the Milwaukee Bucks. Herro always envisioned returning home at some point during his NBA career.

item.title: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough young players and assets to get a deal done." @ShamsCharania says Boston offered Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo before he was traded to Miami 👀

item.title: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: They're very happy that they got Bobby Portis in this trade. They're really excited about how he's gonna fit in with that lineup" - @ramonashelburne

item.title: Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any …
item.description: HoopsHype: New Bucks guard Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any Wisconsin-born player in NBA history. Now you know that.

item.title: Myles Turner feels like a natural trade candidate now.
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Myles Turner feels like a natural trade candidate now. But with the way veterans seem more valued this week post-lottery flattening, perhaps the Bucks keep him to help avoid the relegation zone. Most teams would need to send out $21.6 million to acquire him with his trade bonus.

item.title: Bucks were intrigued by idea of making Jaylen Brown franchise centerpiece
item.description: According to league sources, the Bucks were very intrigued with the idea of making Brown their new franchise centerpiece but also had serious concerns about whether history might repeat itself if he came to town. Brown, the five-time All-Star who is coming off the best season of his career and finished sixth in MVP voting, has three seasons and a combined $183 million left on his current deal. But if he didn’t want to be in Milwaukee, as some signs suggested in recent weeks, then the Bucks might

item.title: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract extension in Milwaukee and he wanted out.. The Bucks were open for business and they're moving forward after making this trade with the Miami Heat" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "In May of 2025 Giannis Antetokounmpo and his agent were very direct with the Bucks that it was time to move on.. He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt like they could no longer compete for a championship" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable …
item.description: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable chatter about the Pistons hoping to get involved in the Antetokounmpo trade to land Tyler Herro, while dangling assets such as Isaiah Stewart, Ron Holland, Duncan Robinson, and draft compensation, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Los Angeles Lakers were linked with trade interest in Kel’el Ware over the past couple of days, sources said.

item.title: If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect …
item.description: Will the Pistons make another run at Herro, who’s currently set to join his hometown Bucks? Or will Detroit attempt to pivot by making a run at Norman Powell in free agency or via trade for Trey Murphy III, whom they’ve expressed interest in? If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect Pelicans executive Troy Weaver to insist on reuniting with Stewart, one of his favorite players from his Pistons tenure.

item.title: NBA executives wondering if Norman Powell will leave Heat after Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: A few executives who spoke with HoopsHype believe Miami’s All-Star guard Norman Powell could now be a candidate to leave the Heat, and the Detroit Pistons are among the teams who’ll be trying to pry him from South Beach, league sources told HoopsHype. Andrew Wiggins has a $30.17 million player option for the upcoming season. Executives are waiting to see if Wiggins will exercise his option or decline it with the intention of signing a new multi-year deal with Miami, or if he’ll consider a new de

item.title: Bucks plan to keep Kel'el Ware for now
item.description: Jake Fischer: I do believe Milwaukee has tried to package Nos. 10 and 13 to move up in tonight's first round, but I don't believe there's going to be any trades in the top-10. Sources say the Bucks, at this point, plan to keep Kel'el Ware, despite interest from rival teams like the Lakers. Milwaukee coveted Ware and Kasparas Jakučionis in their recent drafts. The Bucks also value Tyler Herro, sources say, and don't necessarily plan to move him, while Milwaukee will field offers for the All-Star

item.title: I believe Labaron Philon Jr. is in play at No. 10 and …
item.description: I believe Labaron Philon Jr. is in play at No. 10 and 13 to the Bucks now that Milwaukee controls that pick by way of the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade. Philon told me that the Bucks were the one team he worked out for multiple times and I've heard from multiple sources with knowledge of the Bucks' thinking that they are quite high on the Alabama point guard.

item.title: Myles Turner thought he would be traded to the Celtics and Lakers at different points in his career
item.description: Myles Turner on dealing with constant NBA trade rumors: "I'm so numb to it now. At the very beginning of my career, I was in a trade rumor every single offseason. I had my bags packed twice. I thought at one point in my career I was going to be traded to Boston for Gordon Hayward and I thought I literally was ready to go and then that fell through at the last second. And then I also thought I was going to be traded to the Lakers for a while. So I'm so numb to it now, if it happens it happens and

item.title: Myles Turner: "I've been with my girlfriend for four …
item.description: Myles Turner: "I've been with my girlfriend for four years now and I tell all the time like my life, my career changed when she into my life. It changed my mindset, it changed my mentality. I had the most success I've had in my career with her by my side."

item.title: Myles Turner: "My parents, they're my everything, man. …
item.description: Myles Turner: "My parents, they're my everything, man. They have supported me through and through no matter what decisions I've made in life and they have helped me immensely. They've exemplified what it means like to be parents and to get me to this level. My mom emptied her retirement fund to fund like my AAU. My dad worked the night shift, so I barely saw him during the day, but if I had a game he would sneak out of work, get someone to cover his shift and watch."

item.title: Downtown Milwaukee business district announces more …
item.description: A.J. Bayatpour: Downtown Milwaukee business district announces more than a dozen landmarks and buildings will light up in green tonight to commemorate the time Giannis and Bobby Portis spent in the city. Landmarks include the Hoan Bridge, Mitchell Park Domes and Discovery World

item.title: Brayden Burries headed to Milwaukee at No. 10
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will select Brayden Burries with the No. 10 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Bucks, one day after trading Giannis Antetokounmpo to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bucks, one day after trading Giannis Antetokounmpo to Miami, will be selecting Brayden Burries with the No. 10 pick, sources say.

item.title: Bucks take Nate Ament at No. 13
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will select Nate Ament with the No. 13 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. This pick was acquired from the Miami Heat in the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade.

item.title: But, we're expecting plenty of movement in the second …
item.description: ‪Jake Fischer: Halfway through the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft, we've yet to see a trade of picks outside of Miami sending No. 13 to Milwaukee for Giannis Antetokounmpo. But, we're expecting plenty of movement in the second half, particularly across picks Nos. 20-30.

item.title: "I feel like they're great guys."
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: "I feel like they're great guys." No. 10 overall pick @BraydenBurries shares his reaction to the new-look @Bucks roster with @TermineRadio and @TomCrean.

item.title: I think this has been a great opportunity for us to …
item.description: Eric Nehm: While he couldn't specifically talk about the trade of Giannis Antetokounmpo, Bucks general manager Jon Horst was asked about his vision for the team moving forward and how their two picks tonight fit into that vision. Horst: I think we're always building. We're always looking towards adding positional size. I think that the character component of these two young men we were able to draft matters a ton. I think we talked a lot at Taylor's press conference about style of play and ident

item.title: Myles Turner an option for Raptors?
item.description: NBA sources I’ve spoken with have connected the Raptors to Bucks centre Myles Turner, with the possibility that Raptors centre Jakob Poeltl, third-year wing Gradey Dick and potentially draft compensation being the core of a deal that would net Toronto the veteran big with credentials as a rim protector and three-point threat.

item.title: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete …
item.description: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete a trade for Antetokounmpo is that they resisted the Bucks’ demands that Jaden McDaniels be included in the deal, a team source said. “I think the whole room is excited,” Connelly said. “I think we’re ready to continue to try to take the next step. But Ant’s just such a competitive guy. He wants to make sure we’re moving in the right direction and we keep getting better and better.”

item.title: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis …
item.description: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis whisperer,” a player who had the star’s utmost respect and could deliver hard criticism or honest thoughts about his game. And while Middleton (and the coaching staff) struggled to create cohesion alongside Antetokounmpo and Lillard, Middleton had inherently understood the on-court hierarchy. He may not have liked it, but he long ago decided he would not be the problem. Following his trade, Middleton, bitter, rented a car to drive silently fro

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo reacted to Wes Edens' ultimatum with resignation: 'They're gonna trade me'
item.description: Memories of Game 5 of the 2021 NBA Finals, a win that put the Milwaukee Bucks one game from their championship, coursed through him like the intravenous fluids he needed that night four years earlier. But when a senior team official wandered into view, his mood immediately shifted. Antetokounmpo derisively referenced aloud, without prompt, a comment by Bucks co-owner Wes Edens a day earlier, saying the franchise legend would either sign a contract extension or be traded. Antetokounmpo felt it co

item.title: But multiple high-ranking Bucks officials said …
item.description: Antetokounmpo and Lillard had spoken long before, recruiting one another to their respective cities, and Antetokounmpo famously picked Lillard first for his team in the 2023 All-Star game over Holiday. That's not to say Antetokounmpo demanded the trade for Lillard, or signed off on moving Holiday. But multiple high-ranking Bucks officials said decision-makers would talk with the star, bounce ideas off him. They knew what he wanted, or would be OK with, when it came to coaching or personnel decis

item.title: “Giannis is Milwaukee,” a former teammate said. “When …
item.description: The team was foundering, and they faced more questions about energy and effort. Antetokounmpo and Connaughton openly wondered where the joy had gone. One player said his teammates were still arguing about schemes as they felt the urgency to turn it around. “Giannis is Milwaukee,” a former teammate said. “When you have a guy like Giannis it’s never going to leave your mind of wanting to win a championship. It’s not a thing. There is no such thing of having a good year and figuring it out. It was

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of …
item.description: Then on the team’s media day on Sept. 30, on a Zoom call from Greece because he and the team said he contracted COVID-19, Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of Edens’ statement that they had the on-court “meeting” at the practice facility in early May. Edens was annoyed, but two high-level team sources said he did not carry a grudge. Rivers held another remote training camp in 2025, this time in Miami. Even with a roster that had been completely turned over from the one he took over i

item.title: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker …
item.description: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker room leaks, but his mental state was chronicled nearly all season by anonymous sources. He pleaded for accountability but then tried to pass off those reports as someone else’s doing. He would call his teammates selfish but then stand on a visiting team’s court with a former coach and yell about how that person would make sure he got the ball.

item.title: Kyle Kuzma: Had an amazing time with @Meta speaking …
item.description: Kyle Kuzma: Had an amazing time with @Meta speaking about the future of AI, what I look for in companies, leadership, and how to best serve this new modern world for good through philanthropy. Thanks, @Meta !!!!

item.title: Detroit continues to express trade interest in Tyler Herro
item.description: Marc Stein: With Reaves now sure to stay in LA, as was long believed to be his clear preference, league sources say Detroit continues to express trade interest in Milwaukee's soon-to-be acquired Tyler Herro. The Bucks are expected to listen to offers but could also elect to keep Herro.

item.title: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve …
item.description: Jake Fischer: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve heard they hold some interest in Aaron Nkrumah. But the Grizzlies are indeed seeking more future second-round draft capital. Memphis already netted five second-round picks by maneuvering down the board last night. The Grizzlies, sources say, also received a 2029 most favorable second of Detroit/New York/Milwaukee, a 2031 Dallas second, and a 2032 Detroit second, for sliding from No. 16 to No. 21.

item.title: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim …
item.description: Michael Scotto: FULL TRADE: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim Lopez) and three-second round picks (a 2029 second that’s the second-best of Pistons, Bucks, Knicks, a 2031 Mavericks second, and a 2032 Pistons second) to the Memphis Grizzlies for pick 17 (Ebuka Okorie), per sources.

item.title: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel …
item.description: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel that an earlier construct of the Heat’s proposal for Giannis Antetokounmpo included Davion Mitchell instead of Kasparas Jakucionis. The Bucks, however, insisted on the youthful promise and rookie-scale contract of Jakucionis.

item.title: Warriors wanted Brayden Burries at No. 11
item.description: The expectation: The Bucks would take Nate Ament from Tennessee at 10. So when Atlanta took Houston guard Kingston Flemings at No. 8, and Dallas selected Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. at No. 9, the Warriors thought they had their guy. But having acquired a second lottery pick one day earlier, Milwaukee could be greedy. And it was. The Bucks selected the best player on the board at No. 10 — Arizona guard Brayden Burries — knowing they could get Ament at No. 13. And they did. That’s how close

item.title: One team source indicated that the Warriors had …
item.description: One team source indicated that the Warriors had Lendeborg rated about even with Burries and only had Duke’s Cam Boozer clearly placed on a higher level in this draft.

item.title: The three picks they’re getting back are the three …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Source confirms the Pistons have traded Isaiah Stewart, their longest-tenured player, to the Grizzlies. The three picks they’re getting back are the three they traded last night (2029 2nd that’s the second-best of Pistons, Knicks and Bucks, a 2031 Mavs 2nd and 2032 Pistons 2nd)

item.title: Celtics offers Bucks Jaylen Brown, two first rounders for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Shams Charania: The Celtics aggressively pursued Giannis, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, sources tell ESPN. The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: Miami and Boston, which were both on Antetokounmpo’s list of preferred destinations.

item.title: Milwaukee is acquiring No. 60 from the Magic for cash,
item.description: Shams Charania: Milwaukee is acquiring No. 60 from the Magic for cash, sources said.

item.title: Bucks draft Malique Lewis with the 60th pick
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will select Malique Lewis with the 60th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Bucks also expressed interest in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Jake Fischer: Sources say the Milwaukee Bucks have also registered trade interest recently in LaMelo Ball, but that has dated back to when the Bucks looked at him as a potential addition to Giannis Antetokounmpo. Story here: tinyurl.com/4hxz8593

item.title: “Giannis said so many things,” a former coach said, …
item.description: These deals bothered Doc Rivers and other coaches, as they felt the organization had bowed too much to appease their star player. To them, Giannis Antetokounmpo wanted things both ways. “Giannis said so many things,” a former coach said, “It stems from your actions, which is, 'My brothers have to be on this team.' Well then, are you about a championship? 'Cause they’re not only not helping us win a championship, it’s creating dissension.” Jon Horst, meanwhile, appeared to try to appease Rivers w

item.title: Orlando used the No. 51 pick to select Nelson, the …
item.description: After remaining out of the first round of the NBA draft on Tuesday night, the Magic made multiple trades throughout the second round on Wednesday before ultimately landing on South Florida forward Izaiyah Nelson. Entering the night with only the No. 46 pick, Orlando sent that pick, which was used to select Tennessee forward Felix Okpara, to the Washington Wizards in exchange for the Nos. 51 and 60 draft picks, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Shortly after, the Magic then sent

item.title: Charania: Celtics actively in trade discussions involving Jaylen Brown with multiple teams
item.description: Shams Charania: "Jaylen Brown was offered for Giannis Antetokounmpo, and so that is not the end for him in trade rumors. The Boston Celtics are actively in conversations with interested teams in Jaylen Brown. The sense that I get around the league is that the Celtics are having these discussions with a level of purpose. It does not mean that a trade is guaranteed because Jaylen Brown has three years guaranteed left on his deal on a supermax contract. Finding the right suitor that meets the right

item.title: At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full …
item.description: Beyond a pair of double doors inside the lower-level bowl of Fiserv Forum, a hallway leads to the Bucks locker room. Following the signing of his maximum contract extension in the winter of 2020, it was adorned with Antetokounmpo’s Nike logo, an interlocking “GA." At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full campaign as head coach, the “GA” was removed. Instead, the team emblazoned the word “together” in different languages. The English version of the word was misspelled.

item.title: Having a $90,000 golf simulator for the head coach …
item.description: On its own, a graphic error is not much. Having a $90,000 golf simulator for the head coach take over a communal space in the practice facility is not much. Calling some players into a meeting and not others is not much. Bucks players realized these were not isolated incidents, but a pattern with Rivers, and it diminished their comfort and trust. “Guys had trouble with his delivery,” one player said. “The whole ‘I’ve done this before, this is the way’ approach that was preached to us from Doc wa

item.title: Bucks add Rafael Castro
item.description: Dushawn London: George Washington forward Rafael Castro has signed a two-way deal with the Milwaukee Bucks, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Bucks center Jericho Sims to exercise player option for 2026-27
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Milwaukee Bucks center Jericho Sims plans to exercise his $2.8 million player option for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype . The 27-year-old center started 19 of 67 games for the Bucks, averaging 5.0 points and 5.5 rebounds per game last season.

item.title: Kobe Stewart from Presbyterian will be playing NBA …
item.description: Dushawn London: 6’7” Guard Kobe Stewart from Presbyterian will be playing NBA Summer League with the Milwaukee Bucks. Stewart started over 20 games last season in the G-League and shot 36% from 3. Represented by Keith Kreiter and Sam Cipriano at Edge Sports.

item.title: Kevin Porter exercises his player option to remain in Milwaukee
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: Milwaukee Bucks guard Kevin Porter Jr. is exercising his $5.39 million player option for the 2026-27 season. The 25-year-old guard will remain a key piece for the Bucks in the post Giannis Antetokounmpo era.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was 'genuinely torn' between wanting to be traded or stay in Milwaukee
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo was genuinely torn, multiple sources close to him say. Between loyalty and love of a city that had embraced him as a skinny kid from Greece 13 years ago, and his desire to compete for more championships with a franchise that had exhausted all its resources to try to do that for him. Those contradictory forces had swirled for years.

item.title: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding …
item.description: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding offers and presenting them to his front office and ownership group, then getting feedback from Antetokounmpo's camp as to whether he'd be willing to extend his contract with those teams. At least four strong bids had died on the vine after Antetokounmpo rejected them, multiple sources with knowledge of them say.

item.title: But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple …
item.description: THE BUCKS WERE pressing up against the deadline co-owner Jimmy Haslam had set a month earlier. During a news conference to introduce new coach Taylor Jenkins and sitting alongside Horst, Haslam had made a declaration: The Bucks would have Antetokounmpo's future settled by draft night. Either he would agree to sign an extension to remain with Milwaukee -- or he'd be traded. But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple sources with knowledge of the situation say. Antetokounmpo was not going t

item.title: Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of …
item.description: For half a decade, the Bucks were one of the most successful franchises in the league, leading the NBA in winning percentage (.686) across the regular season and playoffs from 2018 to 2023. Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of the organization through the years: Teams don't willingly trade superstars, especially in a small market such as Milwaukee. It's why the Bucks had made every attempt, through multiple roster iterations, to build championship-level teams with Antetokounmpo

item.title: Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks …
item.description: Later that summer, Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks management their concerns over the roster, and asked the team to explore a trade to the New York Knicks. Talks stalled.

item.title: In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come …
item.description: HORST SLEPT WELL Sunday night and longer than he usually does, relieved to be on the other side of the most consequential decision of his professional life. In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come by. Both franchises believed they had a great chance of landing Antetokounmpo, according to team sources, but they were paralyzed until a final decision had been made.

item.title: Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal
item.description: THROUGHOUT THE DAY on Monday, the Bucks had gone back and forth over the two offers. There were "spirited debates" and differences of opinion, as one Bucks source put it. But in the end, they preferred the optionality that Miami's package gave them in building something new. Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal.

item.title: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from …
item.description: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from Milwaukee back to his native Greece, it seemed to be a signal that the emotional cord between the city and the man who had become a star in it had been severed. Antetokounmpo could never bring himself to publicly ask for a trade, but moving his family to Greece and enrolling his children in Greek schools seemed to be his way of doing so. The move didn't last long, though. Antetokounmpo's family was back in Milwaukee within a few months, multipl

item.title: "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed …
item.description: The second was the inclusion of Holiday in the trade that brought superstar Lillard from Portland. "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed everything was put Jrue in that trade," a Bucks source said. "Jrue was such a leader on and off the floor. He would do things like if Giannis was holding the ball too much, Jrue would just bring it up and play with everybody else without making it a thing. He was a pro at it. "But Jrue was in the deal [for Lillard] because we thought we needed

item.title: Luke Travers will play for the Milwaukee Bucks in the …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Luke Travers will play for the Milwaukee Bucks in the 2026 NBA Summer League, sources told ESPN. The 6'8 Australian forward was the 56th overall pick in the 2022 NBA Draft; he spent the last two seasons on a two-way contract with the Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: Gary Trent Jr expected to enter free agency
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: Milwaukee Bucks sharpshooter Gary Trent Jr. has become a desirable target for teams coveting knockdown shooting to bolster roster. He is expected to decline $3.9M player option to hit open market.

item.title: The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony …
item.description: Portland previously attempted to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, but he communicated that he didn't have interest in signing long-term with the franchise. The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony Davis this offseason as the Washington Wizards consider their options. The Blazers could offer a combination of draft capital, Jerami Grant's expiring contract as salary ballast, and young players such as Scoot Henderson and Sharron Sharpe.

item.title: Myler Turner drawing trade interst
item.description: Sources say that the Bucks have indeed received some trade interest in veteran center Myles Turner in the wake of the Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster, but one league source asserted Friday night that Milwaukee is not actively engaged in moving him.

item.title: Alex Antetokounmpo Greece bound
item.description: Alex Antetokounmpo has officially signed with Promitheas Vikos Cola for the 2026–27 season, the Greek club announced Saturday. This high-profile addition injects significant size and athleticism into the Patras roster for their upcoming campaign. Standing 2.03 meters tall, the versatile 24-year-old forward brings a wealth of global experience to the squad. Born on August 27, 2001, the Greek-Nigerian prospect is the youngest brother of NBA superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo, alongside Thanasis, Kost

item.title: NBA superstar and part-time Las Vegas resident …
item.description: Other notable people involved in groups include Golden Knights owner Bill Foley, who announced his bid officially on Monday, and former Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Marc Lasry. NBA superstar and part-time Las Vegas resident Shaquille O’Neal is also interested in being part of an ownership group, according to his Las Vegas-based business manager Perry Rogers. Rogers said they’ve had conversations with various ownership groups.

item.title: Mavs interested in adding Willie Green to Dusty May's staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Former Pelicans coach Willie Green, who has been pursued by Golden State to rejoin Steve Kerr's staff as an assistant, also has interest from Dallas as new Mavericks coach Dusty May assembles his first NBA staff, league sources say. Former Bucks coach Adrian Griffin and Heat consultant Noah LaRoche have likewise been mentioned as expected candidates for May's bench in Dallas. As @TheSteinLine previously reported, LaRoche was part of the monthlong interview process that eventually led

item.title: Tyler Herro drew trade interest from Lakers and Nets before Bucks trade
item.description: Before Herro joined Milwaukee in the Antetokounmpo trade and the Los Angeles Lakers agreed to a four-year, $185 million max deal with Austin Reaves, the Lakers had trade discussions surrounding Herro and Kel’el Ware, which would’ve involved multiple first-round draft picks, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Brooklyn Nets also did due diligence and gauged the price tag for Herro, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for …
item.description: Trail Blazers All-Star forward Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for Antetokounmpo, and he remains off the table in Brown discussions as of now, league sources told HoopsHype. Portland’s other young players, such as Scoot Henderson and Shaedon Sharpe, are considered available. With Boston considering center upgrades and needing to replace Brown’s position if he’s traded, Portland’s Donovan Clingan and 2025 All-Defensive forward Toumani Camara would both be coveted by Boston. Howeve

item.title: Herro is on an expiring $33 million deal and is …
item.description: Heading into the upcoming season, Herro is on an expiring $33 million deal and is expected to continue to draw trade interest, with many executives around the league curious if Milwaukee will eventually trend more towards a full rebuild or maintain a competitive team with the players acquired for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Aaron Gordon has drawn trade interest from Blazers; Celtics have kept tabs
item.description: Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon was considered among the contingency plans for the Heat if the franchise didn’t land Giannis Antetokounmpo via trade, league sources told HoopsHype. Similarly, the Portland Trail Blazers have expressed trade interest in Gordon should they not land Jaylen Brown, their preferred target, HoopsHype has learned. Ironically, the Celtics have also kept tabs on Gordon from afar.

item.title: Heat linked to Khris Middleton
item.description: Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA forward Khris Middleton, who played with soon-to-be-new-Heat-forward Giannis Antetekounmpo in Milwaukee.

item.title: Team sources said the Bucks would be OK keeping Herro …
item.description: Team sources said the Bucks would be OK keeping Herro on the roster, but execs around the league have told ESPN that Milwaukee has been scouring the trade market to see what it could get in return for him. Flipping Herro for more draft picks or young players could potentially expand the Bucks' return for Antetokounmpo and further their rebuild.

item.title: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit the over on some of his stats. They bet on him to hit the over on rebounds in a March 10 Bucks-Clippers game. He did that in the last few seconds. The group celebrated afterward.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks are declining the team option on Andre …
item.description: Eric Nehm: The Milwaukee Bucks are declining the team option on Andre Jackson Jr. for next season, making him an unrestricted free agent, a team source tells @TheAthletic .

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks declined to tender Ousmane Dieng a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks declined to tender Ousmane Dieng a qualifying offer, which will make him an unrestricted free agent. Bucks have significant interest in re-signing Dieng in free agency. He averaged 11.0 points, 4.6 rebounds and 3.6 assists last season for Milwaukee.

item.title: Taurean Prince has chosen to opt in to his contract …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Taurean Prince has chosen to opt in to his contract for the 2026-27 season and remain with the Milwaukee Bucks, a league source tells @TheAthletic .

item.title: Gary Trent Jr. did not opt in to his contract for the …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Gary Trent Jr. did not opt in to his contract for the 2026-27 season with the Milwaukee Bucks before today's deadline, which makes him an unrestricted free agent, a league source tells @TheAthletic.

item.title: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat, there’s a growing sense within league circles that All-Star guard Norman Powell won’t return. The Chicago Bulls are among the teams expected to pursue Powell as free agency nears, as first reported in my story Sunday.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks are hiring T.J. Ford as part of their …
item.description: Kelly Iko: The Milwaukee Bucks are hiring T.J. Ford as part of their coaching staff, sources tell @YahooSports. Ford, the former Longhorn who played for the franchise from 2003-06, retired from the NBA in 2012.

item.title: Ousmane Dieng signs three-year deal with Bucks
item.description: Free agent forward Ousmane Dieng has agreed to a three-year, $17.5 million deal to return to the Milwaukee Bucks, sources tell ESPN. Dieng had a strong finish to last season after his trade from OKC, and now Bucks officials finalize the deal with agents Michael Tellem of Excel Sports Management and Marius Rutkauskas.

item.title: But he was born in Marrakesh, Morocco, moved to the …
item.description: It is unknown which of those markets Lasry -- who sold the Bucks in 2023 -- is pursuing. But he was born in Marrakesh, Morocco, moved to the U.S. at the age of 7 and, according to sources, is likely not the only former NBA owner to bid for a franchise. Lasry was unavailable for comment.

item.title: Ousmane Dieng and the Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to a …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: Sources: Ousmane Dieng and the Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to a 3-year, $17.5 million deal, including a team option Year 3. Agent Michael Tellem and Marius Rutkauskas of Excel Sports Management did the deal. ESPN was first to report an agreement. HoopsHype first on a team option.

item.title: Bucks are still $33.2M under the luxury tax with an …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: Bucks are still $33.2M under the luxury tax with an estimate plugged in for Ousmane Dieng. Roster spots are a bigger issue than spending power for Milwaukee right now, factoring in the trade with the Heat.

item.title: There’s already a history with both Miami and …
item.description: Heat Central: Shams explains why Miami would be enticing to LeBron: “They have Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo, and Erik Spoelstra. They’re the Miami Heat. There’s already a history with both Miami and Cleveland if LeBron wants to go to either destination and go back to the Eastern conference. But there’s a reason GS formulated a plan over the last week that included Draymond Green surprisingly opting out”

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks have signed Bogoljub Markovic to a …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Milwaukee Bucks have signed Bogoljub Markovic to a second-round pick exception, sources say. Markovic was the Bucks’ No. 47 pick in the 2025 NBA Draft.

item.title: Grizzlies engaged with Bucks on Ja Morant up until Monday
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks, league sources told The Athletic, engaged the Grizzlies on potential trades for Morant as early as January, when it became known Morant was on the market. Those sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe the deliberations, said discussions continued periodically up until Monday, when the Bucks chose not to go through with the trade.

item.title: It’s a four-year, $9.3 million deal with a team option …
item.description: Jake Fischer: It’s a four-year, $9.3 million deal with a team option in the final season.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks have signed 2025 second-round pick …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Milwaukee Bucks have signed 2025 second-round pick Bogoljub Markovic, a league source told @spotrac.

item.title: This is a standard contract for Markovic, likely via …
item.description: Keith Smith: This is a standard contract for Markovic, likely via the Second Round Pick Exception.

item.title: Jonathan Kuminga could land in Milwaukee?
item.description: NBACentral: The Milwaukee Bucks are looming as a potential destination for Jonathan Kuminga, per @JakeLFischer (Via @BleacherReport )

item.title: Jonathan Kuminga drawing interest from Hawks, Cavaliers, Bucks, Kings, Lakers
item.description: Despite helping the Atlanta Hawks win two playoff games against the New York Knicks, the franchise declined Jonathan Kuminga’s $24.3 million team option. Sources say this decision was strategically done to create financial flexibility, including being able to utilize the full non-taxpayer mid-level exception, while still maintaining Kuminga’s Bird Rights, so he can potentially sign a contract at a lower number than his team option amount. It also opens up sign-and-trade possibilities for Atlanta

item.title: The Lakers obviously face financial constraints but …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Lakers obviously face financial constraints but league sources say they continue to explore the feasibility of adding Jonathan Kuminga to fill a wing need. Cleveland and Milwaukee have also expressed Kuminga interest and a return to Atlanta has not been ruled out.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo's upcoming extension was not why Celtics didn't land him
item.description: However, a league source told CelticsBlog that Giannis Antetokounmpo’s looming extension was not a factor in why the Celtics didn’t land him, and that they were prepared to offer him the full 3-year, 35% contract extension.

item.title: The Hawks remain open to re-signing Kuminga, sources …
item.description: The Lakers know, though, that they badly still need more help on the perimeter and were thus trying Wednesday — despite clear financial limitations — to see if a pathway exists to acquiring unrestricted free agent Jonathan Kuminga. The Hawks remain open to re-signing Kuminga, sources say, and there's been interest from Milwaukee and Cleveland along with the Lakers.

item.title: Another hire for Dusty May's first NBA coaching staff: …
item.description: Marc Stein: Another hire for Dusty May's first NBA coaching staff: Joe Boylan. After stints with five other NBA teams, including Memphis, Boylan was initially expected to reunite with Taylor Jenkins on his new Milwaukee staff. But l’m told Boylan is now Dallas-bound along with Willie Green. Joe Boylan will coach the Mavericks’ summer league team.

item.title: Bucks head coach Taylor Jenkins and assistant general …
item.description: According to BasketNews sources, Bucks head coach Taylor Jenkins and assistant general manager Milt Newton are expected to visit Kasparas Jakucionis in Bologna, Italy, where the Lithuanian national team will face Italy in a FIBA World Cup qualifier on Sunday.

item.title: Bucks waive Pete Nance
item.description: The Bucks have waived forward Pete Nance. The team has yet to formally announce the move, but it shows up in the NBA’s official transaction log.

item.title: Bucks, Kam Jones agree to two-way contract
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Milwaukee Bucks and Kam Jones have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told @hoopshype . Jones was the 38th pick of the 2025 NBA Draft. Agent Kevin Bradbury of LIFT Sports Management worked with the Bucks to land Jones in Milwaukee.

item.title: The Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to sign 2025 …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to sign 2025 second-round pick Kam Jones on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Jones was traded from Indiana to Chicago on draft night before being released, and now joins the Bucks.

item.title: He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable …
item.description: James could experience a more organic farewell tour in his third stop in Cleveland, but he has fielded skepticism that he would join the Cavaliers on a veteran’s minimum deal. He’s expected to view the Miami Heat as a more viable option after they acquired Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks. The Heat could also offer a taxpayer mid-level exception between $11 or $12 million per years.

item.title: Brayden Burries signed his rookie deal with the #Bucks …
item.description: ‪Jim Owczarski‬: Brayden Burries signed his rookie deal with the #Bucks this evening - clears the way for him to start playing in summer league games.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo deal between Miami, Milwaukee to remain a two-team trade
item.description: Anthony Chiang: It will be a two-team trade between the Heat and Bucks today, according to a league source. The NBA's moratorium has been lifted since 12:01 p.m.

item.title: Bucks owners release statement on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade as deal becomes official: Forever a Buck
item.description: Shams Charania: Statements from the Milwaukee Bucks on the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat: Bucks owners Wes Edens, Jimmy and Dee Haslam, and Jamie Dinan: “From delivering a championship to transforming our franchise, Giannis' impact on the Milwaukee Bucks is lasting and profound. We are incredibly grateful for what has been a remarkable and historic journey together. For 13 seasons, Giannis gave everything to this organization and inspired our city with his relentless drive, humil

item.title: This was an incredibly difficult decision, made for …
item.description: Shams Charania: Bucks general manager Jon Horst: “Since we drafted him in 2013, Giannis has transformed the Milwaukee Bucks in every way -- on the court, in our locker room and throughout the community. Over 13 seasons, he became an extraordinary leader, teammate and representative of this city, and one of the defining players of his generation. The standard he set will continue here. This was an incredibly difficult decision, made for what is best for the future of the Bucks and for Giannis. On

item.title: This transition also means saying goodbye to another …
item.description: Shams Charania: Jon Horst: This transition also means saying goodbye to another player who played an important role in one of the most successful eras in Bucks history. From the moment Bobby arrived, he embraced what it means to be a Buck and quickly became a fan favorite through his passion, competitiveness and unwavering commitment to winning. Bobby played a vital role in helping our franchise capture the 2021 NBA Championship, and his performance throughout that historic playoff run will fore

item.title: Giannis’ tribute to Milwaukee 💚
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Giannis’ tribute to Milwaukee 💚 (via @Giannis_An34 )

item.title: Pat Riley on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade: One of the great trades in Heat history, Giannis is a Top 5 player in the league
item.description: Your Miami HEAT announced today that they have acquired NBA Champion, Two-Time NBA MVP and 10-time NBA All-Star Giannis Antetokounmpo and NBA Champion Bobby Portis Jr. from the Milwaukee Bucks in exchange for Tyler Herro, Kasparas Jakučionis, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kel’el Ware, three first round picks (2026, 2031 & 2033), a 2030 first round pick swap and a 2033 second round pick. “The announcement of today’s trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis Jr. is one of the great trades in HEAT hist

item.title: “I’m grateful for the Bucks. The fans. This is tough …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis Antetokounmpo live reaction after he was officially traded from the Bucks: “I’m grateful for the Bucks. The fans. This is tough moment for me right now but we’ll get through it. I’m grateful for the fans, memories, championships, my teammates, the city of Milwaukee. The state of Wisconsin”

item.title: Bucks GM Jon Horst, "We're excited to welcome Tyler …
item.description: Ira Windhorst: Bucks GM Jon Horst, "We're excited to welcome Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez and Kasparas Jakucionis to Bucks basketball. Each brings talent, versatility, competitiveness and a team-first mentality that builds our culture and identity."

item.title: From Tyler Herro, "To Spo, Pat, Micky, the entire …
item.description: Ira Winderman: From Tyler Herro, "To Spo, Pat, Micky, the entire organization, my teammates, the coaches, trainers, equipment staff, arena staff, and everyone who poured into me over the last seven years—thank you. Thank you for believing in me, pushing me, challenging me, and helping shape the player and person I’ve become."

item.title: "To Heat Nation, I just want to say thank you for …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Jaime Jaquez Jr. posted this message on his IG today: "To Heat Nation, I just want to say thank you for embracing and supporting me over the past 3 years. The city of Miami will always hold a special place in my heart. Thank you to the coaches and staff as well as the front office for giving me an opportunity to live out my dreams of playing in the NBA. It will always be love Miami."

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo leaves door open for Bucks' return
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo is open to returning to the Bucks later in his career "That would be awesome if I can go and play and maybe come back. Kevin Garnett did the same thing. I saw this clip of him walking into the [Timberwolves'] arena and [the fans] gave him a standing ovation and people to this day love him, and some people don't always feel—" (h/t @BucksLead)

item.title: To recap: On Instagram last week, Herro posted a …
item.description: His off-court activity in the final days before he was shipped to Milwaukee included a classy and thoughtful farewell message on Monday but also included perplexing behavior that triggered a social media backlash days earlier. To recap: On Instagram last week, Herro posted a graphic listing the league’s top worst midrange shooters last season. And there, plain for everyone to see, was Giannis Antetokounmpo listed as the worst (at 26.8%) and Bam Adebayo listed as the 10th worst, at 35.9%. Then an

item.title: Adebayo, incidentally, has never been paid $60 …
item.description: We won’t list the quotes from that private spring conversation because they were shared publicly without his authorization. After the fan seemed to try to incite Herro and mentioned Bam Adebayo favorably, there was a comment — attributed to Herro — wondering whether a top tier defender on “some nights” should get paid $60 million. Adebayo, incidentally, has never been paid $60 million; his current extension tops out with a $57.4 million player option in 2028-29. Herro likely doesn’t want to call

item.title: Caris LeVert traded to Milwaukee
item.description: Shams Charania: The Detroit Pistons are trading Caris LeVert and two second-round picks to the Milwaukee Bucks for Taurean Prince and Gary Harris, sources tell ESPN. Pistons will create a trade exception from the deal and savings while Bucks acquire LeVert and pick up assets.

item.title: Detroit also creates a $15 million trade exception
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Can confirm, per a league source, the Pistons are trading Caris LeVert and two second-round picks to the Bucks for Taurean Prince and Gary Harris. Detroit also creates a $15 million trade exception.

item.title: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via sign-and-trade as part of a larger six-team deal that includes, in total, the Mavericks, Wizards, Clippers, Pistons, Bucks and Grizzlies. It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince (Detroit), Isaiah Stewart (Memphis), Santi Aldama (Mavericks), Caris LeVert (Bucks), etc.

item.title: Bucks get their own second-rounder in 2027 back from …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Update in here on the picks: - Bucks get their own second-rounder in 2027 back from the Pistons and then the least favorable of the Dallas Mavericks’ and Brooklyn Nets’ second-round picks in 2027 as well.

item.title: Kam Jones questioned his NBA future after he was …
item.description: Pacers rookie Kam Jones questioned his NBA future after he was ordered out of his car at gunpoint following a police chase last year, new body cam footage obtained by The California Post shows. Jones’ dramatic encounter with Indiana State Police occurred in Indianapolis on Oct. 10, 2025, after he allegedly hit speeds of over 100 mph and then refused to immediately pull over.

item.title: “I feel very confused right now,” the basketball …
item.description: Cops were seen in the newly released video training their guns on Jones before they demanded he exit his ride and walk toward them slowly. When Jones, 24, ultimately complied and was placed in handcuffs, he insisted he was not trying to evade them on purpose. “I feel very confused right now,” the basketball player said. “Honestly, I saw your right blinker on, so I’m like, ‘Is he trying to get by me?’ So, I was trying to get out your way.” Jones explained he was driving quickly because he was lat

item.title: “I’m going to jail?” he asked. “Yeah,” one cop …
item.description: “I’m going to jail?” he asked. “Yeah,” one cop responded. “Reckless driving and resisting law enforcement.” “I didn’t know you were trying to pull me over, though,” Jones said. “You could have fooled me,” the officer said. As police took Jones toward the back of a squad car, a Pacers assistant coach appeared on the scene. However, cops would not let the two communicate and the Indiana staffer drove off moments later.

item.title: One officer then asked about Jones’ life, and at one …
item.description: One officer then asked about Jones’ life, and at one point, the cop noticed Jones’ 6-foot-3 frame and wondered if he played point guard or shooting guard. Jones then somberly said, “I’m about to be nothing no more.” “Why not?” the cop asked. “I just didn’t realize I was driving that fast,” Jones said.

item.title: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA …
item.description: Since LeBron’s 2020 Lakers title, the last six NBA champions have won without luring accomplished free agents. Instead, the teams have largely drafted stars (Bucks, Warriors, Nuggets, Celtics) or assembled lineups by picking up players other teams were willing to part with (Knicks). The Thunder have built a perennial powerhouse by doing a bit of both, while the Spurs are tied with OKC as 2027/28 favorites thanks to a run of draft success.

item.title: Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the …
item.description: ‪Marc Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks, Wizards and Mavericks is on course to be made official and announced shortly. More NBA from @jakelfischer.bsky.social and me: tinyurl.com/5b4w9jws

item.title: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Per the NBA’s transaction wire, a lot of Pistons trades are now official. They’ve acquired John Collins, Taurean Prince and Gary Harris and traded Isaiah Stewart, Caris LeVert and Marcus Sasser.

item.title: Bucks GM Jon Horst: 'No one's sitting here today and saying that we're a better team today after trading Giannis'
item.description: Milwaukee Bucks general manager Jon Horst called the move to trade Giannis Antetokounmpo a "very difficult decision," but he also acknowledged that it was a transition that was in the best interest of both the Bucks and Antetokounmpo. "No one's sitting here today and saying that we're a better team today after trading Giannis," Horst said during a conference call with reporters Wednesday. "He's one of the greatest players to ever play, greatest player in the franchise history. We've had an incre

item.title: Jon Horst: "For us, it was just about the opportunity …
item.description: Jon Horst: "For us, it was just about the opportunity to build and to again, set a foundation. Can we set something in place with a new coach and take advantage of an opportunity to truly build from a place of strength on a roster that hopefully makes our city proud, our franchise proud and gets us to the place where we are competitive. And we can sustain that competition and that competitive level year in and year out."

item.title: "This was not a 'have to,'" Horst said. "This was, we …
item.description: In the offseason, the Bucks replaced coach Doc Rivers with Taylor Jenkins. Yet Horst denied feeling any pressure that the team was forced to trade Antetokounmpo this summer. "This was not a 'have to,'" Horst said. "This was, we truly believed we've found an opportunity which is unique. We might be right. We might be wrong. But an opportunity where this is what's best for him and what he wants to pursue going forward, and this is what's best for us and what we want to pursue going forward. And th

item.title: Six-team trade involving Mavs, Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks and Wizards made official
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today their participation in a six-team trade involving the Detroit Pistons, L.A. Clippers, Memphis Grizzlies, Milwaukee Bucks and Washington Wizards. Dallas acquired forward Santi Aldama and the rights to forward Tarik Biberović from Memphis along with guard Marcus Sasser from Detroit. Dallas traded guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to Memphis. The Mavericks also sent forward K

item.title: The @Detroit Pistons announced today that the team has …
item.description: The @Detroit Pistons announced today that the team has acquired forward John Collins from the Los Angeles Clippers and forward Taurean Prince and guard Gary Harris from the Milwaukee Bucks as part of a six-team trade.
